---
title: "Customer Retention (churn) Analysis"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
date: "2026-03-29"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Objective

The goal of this project is to identify which customers are more likely to leave (churn) and understand the key factors influencing churn. This analysis will use exploratory data analysis and machine learning techniques to generate insights and predictions.

library(tidyverse)
library(caret)
library(ggplot2)

df <- read.csv("C:/Users/ashle/Downloads/CustomerChurnData.csv")
head(df)

## Data Cleaning

df$TotalCharges <- as.numeric(df$TotalCharges)
df <- na.omit(df)

df$customerID <- NULL

## Convert Data Types 

df[] <- lapply(df, function(x) {
  if(is.character(x)) as.factor(x) else x
})

## Exploratory Data Analysis 

ggplot(df, aes(x = Churn)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue") +
  ggtitle("Churn Distribution")
  
ggplot(df, aes(x = Churn, y = MonthlyCharges)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  ggtitle("Monthly Charges vs Churn")
  
## Contract type vs Churn 

ggplot(df, aes(x = Contract, fill = Churn)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  ggtitle("Churn by Contract Type")
  
## Train/Test Split 

set.seed(42)

trainIndex <- createDataPartition(df$Churn, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train <- df[trainIndex, ]
test <- df[-trainIndex, ]

## Logistic Regression Model 

model <- glm(Churn ~ ., data = train, family = "binomial")
summary(model)

## Predictions 

prob <- predict(model, test, type = "response")
pred <- ifelse(prob > 0.5, 1, 0)

## Risk Scoring 

risk_score <- prob * 100

risk_category <- ifelse(risk_score < 30, "Low",
                  ifelse(risk_score < 70, "Medium", "High"))

results <- data.frame(
  Actual = test$Churn,
  Predicted = pred,
  RiskScore = risk_score,
  RiskCategory = risk_category
)

head(results)

## Risk Visualization

ggplot(results, aes(x = RiskCategory)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "tomato") +
  ggtitle("Customer Risk Categories")
  
## Model Insights

- The model successfully identifies many customers at risk of churn.
- However, some customers who actually churned were classified as low risk.
- This suggests the model may need improvement in identifying subtle churn patterns.
